concepts_list.name: Omega_d
concepts_list.justification: Segment 1, line 13 refers to a partition of the domain into subdomains Omega_i.

concepts_list.name: H_0_1_Omega
concepts_list.justification: Segment 1, line 16 and Segment 2, line 28 describe the spatial domain and its elements as members of H_0^1(Omega).

concepts_list.name: H_1
concepts_list.justification: Segment 2, line 33 refers to the domain as H^1(T; H_0^1(Omega)).

concepts_list.name: u
concepts_list.justification: Segment 1, line 1 refers to ‘u’ as the dependent variable and throughout the text, 'u' is used to represent the solution.

concepts_list.name: cal_T
concepts_list.justification: Segment 1, line 21 refers to the temporal domain T.

concepts_list.name: Omega
concepts_list.justification: Segment 1, line 1 refers to the main spatial domain Omega.

concepts_list.name: t
concepts_list.justification: Segment 1, line 27 refers to the temporal variable t.

concepts_list.name: w
concepts_list.justification: Used in integration by parts in Eq. (8).

concepts_list.name: rho
concepts_list.justification: Segment 2, line 2 refers to the density rho.

concepts_list.name: T
concepts_list.justification: Segment 2, line 2 refers to the tensile pre-stress T.

concepts_list.name: delta_Omega
concepts_list.justification: Segment 1, line 13 refers to the partition of the spatial domain into an arbitrary number of open subdomains.

concepts_list.name: g
concepts_list.justification: Segment 4b, line 38 refers to the load g.

concepts_list.name: Gamma
concepts_list.justification: Segment 8, line 58 refers to the collection of interfaces Gamma.

new_concept_info.description:
Space H_0^1(Omega)
JUSTIFICATION:
Segment 1, line 16 and Segment 2, line 28 describe the spatial domain and its elements as members of H_0^1(Omega).

Space H^1(T; H_0^1(Omega))
JUSTIFICATION:
Segment 2, line 33 refers to the domain as H^1(T; H_0^1(Omega)).

Dependent variable u
JUSTIFICATION:
Segment 1, line 1 refers to ‘u’ as the dependent variable and throughout the text, 'u' is used to represent the solution.

Temporal domain T
JUSTIFICATION:
Segment 1, line 21 refers to the temporal domain T.

Main computational domain Omega
JUSTIFICATION:
Segment 1, line 1 refers to the main spatial domain Omega.

Time
JUSTIFICATION:
Segment 1, line 27 refers to the temporal variable t.

Test function w
JUSTIFICATION:
Used in integration by parts in Eq. (8).

Density rho
JUSTIFICATION:
Segment 2, line 2 refers to the density rho.

Tensile pre-stress T
JUSTIFICATION:
Segment 2, line 2 refers to the tensile pre-stress T.

A single subdomain
JUSTIFICATION:
Segment 1, line 13 refers to the partition of the spatial domain into an arbitrary number of open subdomains.

Load g
JUSTIFICATION:
Segment 4b, line 38 refers to the load g.

Interface Gamma
JUSTIFICATION:
Segment 8, line 58 refers to the collection of interfaces Gamma.
